source: Endgame MCP (get_interaction_history + query_data), keyed against Pulse Google Sheet 18-char opp IDs
note: v2 cache. Prioritizes opps with Discovery Date in last week (5/4-5/10) since that's the default Pulse Deals Created period. Top 5 open opps by amount. Phase 2 will automate this via Vercel cron and key against the live Pulse data source to avoid Endgame-BQ-vs-Sheet ID drift (the v1 cache hit that bug; only Caldwell matched).

latestSignal.speaker: Perry Patton (Director IT)
latestSignal.quote: We are reviewing some additional options right now. I will be in touch in the next few weeks to schedule a full demo.

twoThings: Two well-positioned contacts: Perry Patton (Director of IT, role started March 2026) and Terry McCandless (32 years, runs purchasing and warehousing). Perry explicitly considered building it themselves before deciding to evaluate.
Today's 5/11 email is a yellow flag: 'reviewing additional options' + 'in touch in the next few weeks.' Soft on momentum, deal could drift while they shop.
coachTheRep: Ask Perry: 'Who else are you evaluating, and what would make a difference between options?' Get the competitive set on paper.
Pin the next demo to a date within 14 days. 'A few weeks' from a new IT director with no compelling event is how Q2 becomes Q3.

latestSignal.speaker: Seth Sherman (Warehouse Manager)
latestSignal.quote: Our owner is very much a 'how much is it going to cost me, how much is it going to save me' kind of guy. Things like this are typically a very long process.

twoThings: 10 meetings since March, but most are cold dials with Samuel Shanks (gatekeeper) or VP Sandy Nave's quick brush-offs. Seth Sherman is the one real conversation — Warehouse Manager, not EB.

Seth himself flagged that the owner is cost-focused and the decision cycle is long. Pricing positioned mid-to-high five figures with a 10x ROI claim — but no quantified ROI for Nor-Well specifically yet.
coachTheRep: Ask Seth: 'What does our path to the owner look like? Can you set up a 15-min intro?'
Build the prospect-specific ROI for Nor-Well before the next demo — generic 10x claims won't survive a cost-focused owner conversation.

latestSignal.speaker: McKenna Roy (Lee)
latestSignal.quote: I'll just forward it to the rest of them, OK? [referring to 8 people on the deep-dive demo invite list]

twoThings: Strongest deal of the lastWeek cohort. ABC Convention origin, Ted Brown (Chief Sales Officer) and Andrew Keilman (Sr Manager Ops) both on the 5/5 intro. CSO presence on a $99K opp is unusually senior for this size.

Group demo with 8 people being scheduled for next week (per McKenna Roy on 5/8). When 8 stakeholders show up to a deep dive, the right move is to lock the decision sequence before the demo, not after.
coachTheRep: Before the 8-person demo: ask Ted Brown 'If this lands well, who else needs to weigh in and what's the next step on your side?'
Confirm whether this is one decision or several. Materials Buyer (Scott Waple), COA (Krista Fish), CSO, Sr Ops Mgr — different functions, possibly different criteria.

latestSignal.speaker: Igor Fuentes
latestSignal.quote: I wanted to come back to the C suite with a little bit more information around what else is available besides what we're looking at right now. You'll hear from me — yay/nay or next steps — probably within a week.

twoThings: Trimble Materials displacement play. SFDC NextStep confirms it: 'mid implementation with Trimble and it's going poorly.' Igor Fuentes shared $90M revenue / $30M material spend / 2,600 POs/year — real numbers, real diligence.
Igor is acting like a buyer (asking pricing, API docs, PowerBI integration) AND naming the C-suite as the decision body. 'Decision within a week' is a soft compelling event we should sharpen.
coachTheRep: Ask Igor: 'When you bring this back to the C-suite, who specifically is on that call, and what would tip them to yes vs no?' Get the EB named before the team demo next week.
Position the Trimble switching cost: what does Schwob lose if they keep going down the failing implementation? Quantify the cost of inaction.

latestSignal.speaker: Brett Steinkamp (K&W)
latestSignal.quote: The biggest question they're going to ask me is OK, looks good. How much does it cost?

twoThings: Foundation ERP integration confirmed — that's the technical green light. Brett Steinkamp is engaged and openly relaying his upper management's questions, which is champion-coded behavior.

But Brett has flagged that price is the make-or-break question for the people above him, and we don't know those people. Marty is preparing ROI and pricing — that's the right work but it lands cold without an EB intro.
coachTheRep: Ask Brett: 'Who specifically signs off on a $40-50K SaaS tool? Can I be in the room when you present the pricing, or send a one-pager you can hand them?'
K&W is $28M annual revenue — frame ROI as $/foreman/year and material waste avoided, not generic 2-5%.
